name: Component extraction seam and registry
summary: Create the shared code seam that every Mission Control page consumes before page-specific work begins.
deliverable: Introduce a stable component registry/contract for the mini app primitives currently scattered across `workers/quests/src/page.ts`, with named helpers, shared prop shapes, data-component attributes, and source-reference comments that point back to the modular component map.
acceptance: Pages can render primitives only through named component helpers or a deliberate registry wrapper.

The registry covers MissionGlyph, StateToken, OrbitProgress, SelectedHalo, SignalRail, PacketFlow, BranchArcChip, MissionCard, QuestlineTimeline, ProofList, KpiPulse, GateActionRow, and the motion primitives.
The contract keeps raw architecture/source/schema wording out of primary page primitives and leaves Inspect/detail sheets as the allowed debug surface.

name: Cambium visual token layer
summary: Build reusable CSS tokens for the extracted component system.
deliverable: Extract the deep teal shell, chartreuse active accent, soft mint copy, peach warning accent, contour texture, safe-area spacing, radius, border, and density rules into a reusable mini app token layer consumed by every component primitive.
acceptance: Component CSS uses shared token names rather than page-local one-off colors.
8px-or-smaller component radius, Telegram safe-area spacing, and thumb-safe action sizing are available as reusable rules.
The palette stays Cambium-native and does not drift into purple/blue AI gradients, generic SaaS cards, or single-hue surfaces.

name: MissionGlyph inline SVG variants
summary: Replace Unicode placeholder glyphs with real reusable inline SVG glyphs.
deliverable: Build `MissionGlyph` as a text-accessible ornamental primitive with inline SVG variants for genesis, taste, build, ops, cortex, arc, proof, and gate, mapped to the component board and Cambium visual stage contract.
acceptance: No glyph variant renders as a bare text character such as star, triangle, circle, or crescent placeholder.
Active glyphs can receive a chartreuse halo and one orbit ring; warning glyphs use peach stroke with no glow bloom; locked glyphs desaturate at low opacity.
Glyphs remain ornamental identity only and do not become standalone buttons without adjacent accessible labels.

name: StateToken taxonomy and renderer
summary: Build the compact state token primitive used by every page.
deliverable: Implement `StateToken` with explicit idle, active, selected, complete, blocked, locked, stale, proof-needed, and reducedMotion states, including icon/label support so color is never the only state cue.
acceptance: State mapping is centralized and pages do not invent extra token classes ad hoc.
Blocked and stale states are visually distinct from complete/ready states.
Reduced-motion state renders as a static visible label instead of removing the state treatment.

name: OrbitProgress primitive
summary: Build the reusable circular progress indicator for questlines and KPI pulse.
deliverable: Implement `OrbitProgress` with value, state, label, optional packet dots, active partial arc, complete full ring, blocked peach segment, stale treatment, and dotted pending proof ring.
acceptance: Progress values clamp to 0..100 and always render a readable label.
Blocked/proof-needed progress cannot look like successful completion.
MissionCard, KpiPulse, Gate orbit, and proof states can share the primitive rather than maintaining separate ring implementations.

name: SelectedHalo primitive
summary: Build one reusable selected-state treatment for chips, nodes, and sheets.
deliverable: Implement a `SelectedHalo` primitive or CSS wrapper that supports one clean orbit/underline selection treatment, transform/opacity-only animation, and static reduced-motion rendering.
acceptance: Only one selected item per rail receives the selected halo at a time.
The selected treatment is not duplicated through competing borders, glows, and underlines in the same component.
BranchArcChip, MissionStateStack rows, and detail-sheet headers can share the same selected visual language.

name: SignalRail component states
summary: Build the rail primitive that connects mission nodes and branch states.
deliverable: Implement `SignalRail` with idle, active, blocked, and locked states, including muted mint line, chartreuse active rail, dashed blocked/locked variants, warning endpoint, and page-safe sizing.
acceptance: Packet dots travel only along rails and never cover text.
Blocked rails terminate in a warning token rather than a success marker.
QuestlineTimeline, MissionCard texture, Story timeline, and Inspect rail panels can consume the same rail contract.

name: PacketFlow dot stream
summary: Build the tiny packet-dot primitive used sparingly for live movement.
deliverable: Implement `PacketFlow` as a bounded dot stream with count limits, state-aware opacity/color, static reduced-motion trail, and no text overlap.
acceptance: Packet dots appear only inside rails, map texture, or explicit packet bars.
Reduced motion freezes the dots into a static trail without removing evidence of flow.
Dot count, spacing, and state are reusable inputs rather than hard-coded per page.

name: BranchArcChip primitive
summary: Build the branch selector chip as a reusable component.
deliverable: Implement `BranchArcChip` with MissionGlyph, branch name, short mission copy, optional StateToken, selected halo support, stale/blocked/locked treatment, and tap-safe sheet routing hooks.
acceptance: Fitcheck selected active, Vantyx stale, Snow Gloves blocked, and IVerif locked can all render from the same component API.
Long branch/mission names truncate cleanly without shifting chip height or clipping the state token.
The chip exposes stable data attributes for click handlers and viewport proof selectors.

name: MissionCard composite primitive
summary: Build the dominant next-mission card as a reusable composite.
deliverable: Implement `MissionCard` with Next Mission eyebrow, title, owner, gate, dispatch target, promotion state, OrbitProgress, branch map/rail texture, and product-facing summary copy.
acceptance: MissionCard copy remains founder-readable and avoids raw source/schema/envelope/quest-ledger language.
The rail/map texture uses real SignalRail and PacketFlow primitives rather than decorative one-off styling.
The card can be consumed by Mission and branch detail sheets with consistent state styling.

name: QuestlineTimeline primitive
summary: Build the four-to-six stage mission timeline component.
deliverable: Implement `QuestlineTimeline` with stage labels, MissionGlyph, StateToken, SignalRail connectors, packet-active state, blocked warning endpoint, and locked continuation.
acceptance: Seed complete, Packet active, Proof blocked, and Launch locked can render as the canonical four-stage phone reference.
The timeline accepts four to six stages without layout overflow on mobile.
Stage labels are product-facing and raw architecture detail moves to Inspect or sheets.

name: ProofList primitive
summary: Build proof-needed rows as a reusable founder-facing list.
deliverable: Implement `ProofList` rows with proof glyph/orbit state, proof-needed/blocked/stale distinctions, concise outcome copy, chevron/tap affordance, and detail-sheet hooks.
acceptance: Rows describe founder-understandable proof needs instead of raw paths, routes, or schema IDs.
Proof-needed rows use dotted receipt/orbit treatment and cannot appear complete until evidence exists.
Mission, Gate, Story, and Inspect linkouts can reuse the row API with appropriate copy containment.

name: KpiPulse primitive
summary: Build KPI pulse rows with orbit rings and packet bars.
deliverable: Implement `KpiPulse` with compact KPI label, OrbitProgress ring, packet-bar signal depth, survival/better-than-survival copy, stale/blocked treatment, and reduced-motion stability.
acceptance: KPI rows no longer use two-letter placeholder circles as the primary visual.
Signal depth is represented without implying fake progress or success.
Mission and branch sheets can share the same KPI primitive.

name: GateActionRow primitive
summary: Build non-floating action rows for Review Gate and Open Proof.
deliverable: Implement `GateActionRow` with stable in-flow layout, thumb-friendly buttons, state-aware disabled/pending styling, haptic/tap-safe attributes, and sheet-routing integration.
acceptance: Review Gate and Open Proof never float over content or block scroll/swipe flow.
Buttons retain current click handlers and open the correct gate/proof sheets.
The action row can appear in Mission empty states and detail sheets without changing its interaction contract.

name: orbitSweep motion primitive
summary: Build the calm selected/progress orbit animation primitive.
deliverable: Implement `orbitSweep` as reusable CSS animation or class contract for active OrbitProgress and selected branch chips, with slow cubic easing and static reduced-motion fallback.
acceptance: Only the current focal component uses orbitSweep at a time.
Reduced motion renders a static partial ring with the same state label.
The animation does not trigger aggressive pulse, sparkle, or layout movement.

name: packetDrift motion primitive
summary: Build the rail-confined packet movement primitive.
deliverable: Implement `packetDrift` for PacketFlow inside SignalRail and branch textures, with bounded speed, sparse dots, and reduced-motion static trail.
acceptance: Packet motion stays confined to rail paths and does not cross text or buttons.
The effect is subtle enough for Telegram mini app use and repeat sessions.
Story, Mission, and Inspect can share the same motion class without page-local animation definitions.

name: glyphBreathe motion primitive
summary: Build the subtle current-glyph emphasis primitive.
deliverable: Implement `glyphBreathe` for the current branch/stage glyph only, with a small scale range, no warning pulse, and reduced-motion static treatment.
acceptance: Blocked, warning, stale, and locked glyphs do not breathe or pulse.
The current glyph emphasis is subtle and does not compete with selected halo/orbitSweep.
The primitive is opt-in by state and cannot be accidentally applied to every glyph on a page.

name: warningAttention motion primitive
summary: Build the single-pass Gate/proof warning attention treatment.
deliverable: Implement `warningAttention` for blocked proof or Gate review rows, with peach stroke appearing once and resting in a readable review state.
acceptance: Warnings do not flash, loop aggressively, or masquerade as success accents.
Reduced motion renders the resting review state immediately.
Gate, ProofList, State Stack, and Gate decision cards can reuse the same warning treatment.

name: Reduced motion and accessibility contract
summary: Build the accessibility contract every primitive must honor.
deliverable: Implement a shared reduced-motion/static-state contract, text-first labels, aria labels where needed, non-color-only state cues, and stable dimensions for component primitives.
acceptance: Every primitive remains readable with reduced motion enabled.
State is communicated by label/icon/shape, not color alone.
Long labels and mobile widths do not cause text overlap or layout jumps.

name: Component gallery proof surface
summary: Build a proofable component gallery/fixture before page execution starts.
deliverable: Add a lightweight component gallery or proof fixture that renders all extracted primitives, canonical states, and motion-static frames from the modular component references, using stable selectors for viewport proof.
acceptance: Gallery/proof includes glyph variants, state tokens, orbit progress states, mission composites, and motion storyboard static states.
Page work #100-#199 can reference the gallery as the visual lock before integrating primitives into Mission, Gate, Tools, Story, and Inspect.
The proof surface is implementation-facing and does not create a new production page unless routed behind an explicit proof/debug flag.
